name: Pitjantjatjara

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Pitjantjatjara (pitj1243).

notes: Pama-Nyungan (Wati) language of Central Australia, with a well-established standard orthography. Retroflex consonants are written with an underline (ṯ ṉ ḻ ṟ); the common ASCII substitutions rt/rn/rl are also mapped to the same retroflex values. The palatal series is tj/ny/ly, ng is the velar nasal, single r is an alveolar trill/tap and ṟ the retroflex approximant. Long vowels are written by doubling (aa ii uu). Word-initial stress and the intervocalic voicing of stops (unmarked in spelling) are left unencoded.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Pitjantjatjara (pitj1243)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology

sources.author: Wikipedia contributors
sources.title: Pitjantjatjara dialect
sources.notes: Phonology and standard orthography reference for the grapheme map.
